perturbation.original_text: "Affiliate Advertising Share" has the meaning set forth in Exhibit D.
perturbation.changed_text: "Affiliate Advertising Share" has the meaning subject to Affiliate's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying the definition of 'Affiliate Advertising Share' from being explicitly defined in Exhibit D to being subject to 'Affiliate's discretion', it introduces significant uncertainty. This contradicts the existing structure where the share is intended to be calculated based on objective criteria outlined in Exhibit D, and the financial obligations now become unclear. The in-text contradiction arises because the rest of the contract continues to refer to 'Affiliate Advertising Share' as if it were a fixed, determinable quantity, when the new definition suggests it is subject to the Affiliate's unilateral control.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) In consideration of the terms and conditions set forth herein, Network shall pay Affiliate (i) the Affiliate Advertising Share, and (ii) the Affiliate Transactional Share, each as provided in Exhibit D.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly references Exhibit D for the definition of "Affiliate Advertising Share", establishing an objective, defined calculation. The changed text introduces subjective discretion by stating the term is 'subject to Affiliate's discretion'. This contradicts section (b) which states 'Network shall pay Affiliate (i) the Affiliate Advertising Share... as provided in Exhibit D,' because the Affiliate Advertising Share is no longer objectively defined within Exhibit D, thus creating a direct conflict and contradiction, and uncertainty regarding payment obligations. The introduction of Affiliate's discretion directly contradicts the intention of a predetermined amount as indicated in Exhibit D.

perturbation.original_text: (g) Each Station, by the terms of this contract, shall be entitled to invoke the protection against duplication of Service programming imported under the compulsory copyright license as provided in Sections 76.101 and 76.123 of the FCC Rules.
perturbation.changed_text: (g) Each Station, by the terms of this contract, may consider copyright protections.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall be entitled to invoke the protection against duplication of Service programming' to 'may consider copyright protections', it introduces ambiguity and undermines the station's entitlement to copyright protection. The modification contradicts the implicit assumption in the contract that stations are afforded specific protections under FCC rules, leading to uncertainty in enforcement of copyright-related provisions. Other clauses assume this protection exists, so the change generates internal contradiction. Also the original sections reffered to FCC Rules, making it a strong entitlement, while the change gives the station the power to consider what to do.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (e) Without limiting Section 9(d), Network shall indemnify, defend and forever hold harmless the Affiliate Indemnitees from and against any and all Costs arising directly or indirectly out of third-party claims (i) that the transmission by Affiliate of the Service as contemplated herein infringes the rights of any person, (ii) based on the content of the Service and any promotional material provided by Network to Affiliate (including the Promotional Spots), as furnished by Network and transmitted by Affiliate and each Station in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement (i.e., not based upon any deletions, modifications or additions by Affiliate or any Station), including any claim that such content or material is obscene, indecent, libelous, or slanderous, or violates any right of privacy or publicity, copyright, trademark or any other proprietary, literary, or dramatic right of any person or any rule or regulation of the FCC, and (iii) relating to any contest, sweepstakes or other promotion conducted by Network.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that each station 'shall be entitled to invoke the protection against duplication of Service programming' under specific FCC rules. The changed text weakens this entitlement to 'may consider copyright protections,' introducing ambiguity and uncertainty. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes a strong copyright protection is in place, particularly regarding Network's responsibility to indemnify and defend against third-party claims of infringement due to the transmission of the Service. The weakened copyright protection directly undermines the Network's indemnification obligations and creates an inconsistency in the agreement concerning copyright enforcement.

perturbation.original_text: Network also shall display and encode program ratings for the Service using the industry-standard "V-chip" ratings system.
perturbation.changed_text: Network may consider displaying program ratings for the Service.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the requirement for Network to display and encode program ratings using the V-chip system introduces uncertainty regarding compliance with industry standards and parental control mechanisms. The in-text contradiction manifests if another section references Network's adherence to industry standards or if Affiliate relies on program ratings for content suitability assessments. This affects parental control measures to the TV program, not making the TV program reliable for children, since there are no industry-standard V-chip requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) Children's Programming. (i) Network will provide as part of the Service the minimum number of hours of "Core Programming," as defined in 47 C.F.R. §73.671(c), as the same may be amended from time to time ("Core Children's Programming"), and will comply with related requirements of the definition of "Core Programming" in order to enable Affiliate to comply with the "safe harbor" established by law or FCC regulation, solely with regard to the Service and as a result of the broadcast by the Stations of the Service on each such Station's free, over-the-air, multicast feed.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated the use of the industry-standard "V-chip" ratings system. The changed text makes the use of program ratings discretionary ("may consider"). The contradicted text specifies that Network will provide 'Core Programming' and comply with related requirements to enable Affiliate to comply with the 'safe harbor' established by law or FCC regulation. Because the modified text removes the mandate to utilize industry standard ratings and parental control mechanisms, compliance with the safe harbor provisions would be put in jeopardy, directly contradicting the expectations in the contradicted text.
